title: rosa arkansana f. alba
intro: Rosa arkansana f. alba, also known as the Arkansas rose or prairie rose, is a white-flowering variant of the Arkansas rose plant, a shrub native to the prairies and open woods of the central United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rosa arkansana f. alba include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Rosa arkansana f. alba can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine to support skin health and soothe irritated skin.
